children.heading: Deep-draft harbor
children.label: 1 Deep-draft harbor
children.text: The term “deep-draft harbor” means a harbor which is authorized to be constructed to a depth of more than 45 feet (other than a project which is authorized by ).section 202 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), the term “eligible operations and maintenance” means all Federal operations, maintenance, repair, and rehabilitation, including (i) maintenance dredging reasonably necessary to maintain the width and nominal depth of any harbor or inland harbor; (ii) the construction of dredged material disposal facilities that are necessary for the operation and maintenance of any harbor or inland harbor; (iii) dredging and disposing of contaminated sediments that are in or that affect the maintenance of Federal navigation channels; (iv) mitigating for impacts resulting from Federal navigation operation and maintenance activities; and (v) operating and maintaining dredged material disposal facilities.

children.label: B
children.text: As applied to the Saint Lawrence Seaway, the term “eligible operations and maintenance” means all operations, maintenance, repair, and rehabilitation, including maintenance dredging reasonably necessary to keep such Seaway or navigation improvements operated or maintained by the Great Lakes St. Lawrence Seaway Development Corporation in operation and reasonable state of repair.

children.label: C
children.text: The term “eligible operations and maintenance” does not include providing any lands, easements, or rights-of-way, or performing relocations required for project operations and maintenance.

children.heading: General cargo harbor
children.label: 3 General cargo harbor
children.text: The term “general cargo harbor” means a harbor for which a project is authorized by and any other harbor which is authorized to be constructed to a depth of more than 20 feet but not more than 45 feet;section 202 of this title

children.label: D
children.text: channels or harbors constructed or maintained by nonpublic interests; and

children.label: E
children.text: any portion of the Columbia River other than the channels on the downstream side of Bonneville lock and dam.

children.label: B
children.text: projects that are subject to tidal influence;

children.label: C
children.text: projects with authorized depths of greater than 20 feet;

children.label: D
children.text: local access or berthing channels; and

children.label: E
children.text: projects constructed or maintained by nonpublic interests.

children.heading: Nominal depth
children.label: 6 Nominal depth
children.text: The term “nominal depth” means, in relation to the stated depth for any navigation improvement project, such depth, including any greater depths which must be maintained for any harbor or inland harbor or element thereof included within such project in order to ensure the safe passage at mean low tide of any vessel requiring the stated depth.

children.heading: Non-Federal interest
children.label: 7 Non-Federal interest
children.text: The term “non-Federal interest” has the meaning such term has under and includes any interstate agency and port authority established under a compact entered into between two or more States with the consent of Congress under section 10 of Article I of the Constitution.section 1962d–5b of title 42

children.heading: United States
children.label: 8 United States
children.text: The term “United States” means all areas included within the territorial boundaries of the United States, including the several States, the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, the Virgin Islands, Guam, American Samoa, the Trust Territory of the Pacific Islands, the Northern Mariana Islands, and any other territory or possession over which the United States exercises jurisdiction.
